All right, let's talk about mute outs and getting link click triggers to work in your MVL sequence. So for this demonstration, we are going to use example one, the first prelaunch email sent in the sequence. And this is the copy school Black Friday 2020 example. And so if you scroll down in your workbook, you'll see at the bottom here, there are the two links, right? The red pill, the blue pill. So we have our blue linkage, which is going to give them that kind of surprise first access to the offer and trigger the triggered email. And then of course, we have the red link, which is the mute out, which is going to remove them from the automated campaign so that they no longer continue to get sales emails related to the software. So what do we need to do here? We need to set the link trigger actions for both of these. And what I'm going to show you here will be true for any of the link clicks that you do throughout this campaign. And so what I'm going to do is I'm just going to copy this little chunk of this. I'm not going to take the whole email. And we're going to go into our MVL 2.0 launch sequence that you should have imported by now and go to day one, PL one. And I'm going to edit this. And we'll just paste this in here so you can see what this looks like. Let's make it a little bit bigger. So it's a little easier to read. And let's do the link click trigger first. So what you're going to want to do here is you're going to want to highlight the link. Set it by clicking the little link chain here. And I'm just going to send this to Google, but this would be linked to your sales page that you're promoting. But the thing that's most important here is this, which is when a contact clicks this link at an action and what action do we want to add? Well, we want to add a tag. If you remember in my videos for the triggered automations, we have a trigger tag that will trigger that email to go out. So we're simply going to search for that. And because I've never used it, it's not showing up here. But what I can do to show you this is if we go back, let me open up a new tab here. This is the best way to do it actually. So you make sure because these are case sensitive. So since this is the first email, this is the email that's going to trigger our MBL 1A. So we want to make sure that we set that trigger tag. So here you can see that the start is this trigger 1A early access. That's the tag we actually need to add. So we're going to copy that to my clipboard come over here. And that is the tag that we want to add. So you can see when they click that link, which would be going to your hypothetical sales page, add the trigger tag for that early access with ex-bio-code. That's the triggered email 1A and click OK. Active campaign is a little wonky. Sometimes the color doesn't show up. So we'll just make sure that looks right. And that's it. So now when someone clicks that email or when someone clicks that link, active campaign will be added. Active campaign will register that. Of course, it will send them to the sales page, but it will also queue them up to receive that triggered email 15 minutes later. And so when you're setting up triggered email 1B, which will be in a subsequent pre-launch email, you will do the same thing except you will use the trigger tag from that automation, which would be a separate trigger tag. So that's basically it for your triggered emails. And as far as the do not disturb function here, we'll set that to a red link. And yeah, you can send these folks wherever you want. I like to set up kind of like a kind of fun little page that might be like an opt-in for something else or just like we've done a page with like a an office video before, which is closing time. And it was just playing that fun YouTube video. So we try to make it a little bit fun, but you can really send them wherever you want. That's up to you. It does have to be a different link than your other other link. So wherever you want to send them. And then what you'll do here is if you remember we have already or should have you should have already created your mute tag. So whatever mute tag for the campaign, that's what you want to add. So you can see when that link is clicked, we're going to add mute and the L 2.0. So it's going to send them to wherever you send them. And then it's going to tag your contact with that mute tag, which means within the triggered email sequences as well as the main email sequence. Those contacts will get filtered out of the automation and will not receive any additional emails. That's really it. Quite simple. Once you know what you're doing, it's a question that comes up often how to set this up. But once you know the trick, you know the trick. And you can implement this anywhere as needed in your funnel. All right. Much love. Have a beautiful day.
